data.body:
IMPLEMENTATION NOTES (fix mechanics experimentally validated in sandbox, 2026-06-09):
Mode parse API: rebar_reconciler/mode.py exposes Mode.from_str(value) -> Mode (classmethod; raises ValueError listing allowed values). Import works in-process in mcp_server.py with NO sys.path work: `import rebar` (already at module top) transitively imports rebar._native which puts the engine dir on sys.path, so `from rebar_reconciler.mode import MODE_CAPS, Mode` resolves. CRITICAL: MODE_CAPS[Mode.LIVE] is None (uncapped) — gate on `!= 0`, NOT `> 0`.
Validated replacement for the mode gate in the reconcile tool body:
    from rebar_reconciler.mode import MODE_CAPS, Mode
    parsed = Mode.from_str(mode)  # ValueError on unknown mode -> clean tool error
    if MODE_CAPS[parsed] != 0:    # any mutating mode (10/100/None)
        if _readonly():
            raise ValueError(f"{parsed.value} reconcile is disabled: this server is read-only (REBAR_MCP_READONLY)")
        if os.environ.get("REBAR_MCP_ALLOW_RECONCILE_LIVE","").strip().lower() not in ("1","true","yes"):
            raise ValueError(f"{parsed.value} reconcile is disabled (mutating mode); set REBAR_MCP_ALLOW_RECONCILE_LIVE=1 to enable")
    return rebar.reconcile(parsed.value)
PINNED WORDING TRAP: tests/interfaces/test_mcp.py::test_live_reconcile_refused_without_optin pins the substring "live reconcile is disabled" — keep "<mode> reconcile is disabled" phrasing or update that test deliberately.
Validated matrix: 4x6 (READONLY x ALLOW_LIVE x five modes + "bogus"), engine monkeypatch-recorder + loud fake acli (never invoked). Baseline confirmed bootstrap-* AND "bogus" reached the engine ungated. Patched: cap-0 modes always allowed; readonly blocks mutating modes EVEN WITH ALLOW_LIVE=1; bogus -> clean error. test_mcp.py + test_surface.py: 11 passed.
Coordination: if landed with bug ship-mogul-glob, this diff already lowercases ALLOW_RECONCILE_LIVE — that ticket's hunk for it is superseded.
